value: Fully integrated commercial-stage innovative oncology company
notes: Per company: an approved next-generation PD-1 inhibitor (LOQTORZI / toripalimab-tpzi) plus a pipeline of two mid-stage clinical candidates (tagmokitug, an anti-CCR8 Treg depleter; casdozokitug, an IL-27 antagonist) targeting liver, prostate, head & neck, colorectal and other cancers.
Single approved/commercial product; anti-PD-1 for R/M nasopharyngeal carcinoma. Company describes itself as commercial-stage oncology company with two mid-stage clinical candidates.

value: Adequately capitalized near-term; profile hinges on LOQTORZI ramp and capital access
source_page_or_section: Liquidity & Risk Factors; Q1 2026 earnings release
notes: Orion Analysis (preliminary): near-term credit position is supported by a net-cash balance sheet and a covenant-light, interest-only 2029 term loan with substantial minimum-liquidity headroom, so default risk over the next ~12 months appears low. Longer-term repayment of the 2029 bullet depends on LOQTORZI revenue growth (single-product concentration) and continued capital-markets access given sustained operating losses (~$36M Q1 2026 operating loss; ~$58M Q1 operating cash use). Outlook: cautiously stable, watch burn rate, LOQTORZI trajectory, and pipeline readouts. Underlying support: 10-Q Liquidity/Risk Factors and cash-flow statement; Q1 2026 earnings release.
